intro: Pedilonum miyakei, also known as Japanese gentian or Miyake's gentian, is a species of flowering plant in the gentian family (Gentianaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments, such as digestive issues and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Pedilonum miyakei include gentiogenin, gentiopicroside, and other iridoid glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Japanese herbal medicine, Pedilonum miyakei is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or powder for use in treating various health conditions.
